examples.title: PUSH Operation in Register Stack
examples.description: Demonstrates how PUSH works in register stack organization with overflow checking
examples.code:
ALGORITHM PUSH_REG_STACK
INPUT: value to push
BEGIN
  IF (stack_pointer >= max_registers) THEN
    // Register stack full - need to spill
    SPILL_TO_MEMORY(stack_pointer, value)
    stack_pointer = stack_pointer + 1
  ELSE
    register_stack[stack_pointer] = value
    stack_pointer = stack_pointer + 1
  END IF
  RETURN stack_pointer
END
examples.explanation: The PUSH operation checks if register stack has space. If full, it spills oldest entry to memory before pushing. Stack pointer is incremented after push.
examples.spaceComplexity: O(n) where n is number of register slots

examples.title: POP Operation in Register Stack
examples.code:
ALGORITHM POP_REG_STACK
BEGIN
  IF (stack_pointer <= 0) THEN
    // Register stack empty - underflow error
    RAISE_UNDERFLOW_ERROR()
    RETURN NULL
  ELSE
    stack_pointer = stack_pointer - 1
    value = register_stack[stack_pointer]
    // Check if we need to fill from memory spill area
    IF (memory_spill_present AND stack_pointer < min_register) THEN
      value = FILL_FROM_MEMORY()
    END IF
    RETURN value
  END IF
END
examples.explanation: POP decrements stack pointer first (for downward-growing stack), retrieves value. If values were spilled to memory, they are restored when needed.

examples.title: Function Prologue and Epilogue
examples.description: Demonstrates stack frame setup and teardown in register stack organization
examples.code:
def function_prologue(param_count, local_count, frame_pointer):
    # Save old frame pointer
    old_fp = frame_pointer
    # Allocate space for locals (simulate decrementing stack pointer)
    new_fp = frame_pointer - 4  # space for saved FP
    # Save callee-saved registers (simulated)
    saved_registers = [0] * 4  # save 4 registers
    # Create stack frame
    stack_frame = {
        'return_address': None,
        'old_fp': old_fp,
        'params': [None] * param_count,
        'locals': [None] * local_count,
        'saved_regs': saved_registers
    }
    return new_fp, stack_frame

def function_epilogue(frame_pointer, return_value, old_fp):
    # Place return value in designated register
    result_register = return_value
    # Restore frame pointer
    restored_fp = old_fp
    # Return to caller
    return result_register, restored_fp

# Example usage
fp, frame = function_prologue(param_count=3, local_count=5, frame_pointer=1000)
print(f"Stack frame created at FP={fp}")
print(f"Frame contains: {len(frame['locals'])} locals, {len(frame['params'])} params")
result, restored_fp = function_epilogue(fp, 42, frame['old_fp'])
print(f"Returned: {result}, Restored FP: {restored_fp}")
examples.explanation: This code demonstrates the Prologue (setting up stack frame, saving FP) and Epilogue (returning value, restoring FP) phases of function execution.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n) where n is local variable count

examples.title: Register Stack with Spilling Implementation
examples.description: C implementation showing register stack with automatic spilling to memory
examples.explanation: This C implementation demonstrates register stack with automatic spilling to memory when all registers are full. It shows push/pop operations, overflow handling through memory spilling, and underflow detection.
examples.timeComplexity: O(1) for register operations, O(n) for spill operations
